_note: Synthetic test-screening and social feedback for the fictional slate. Demo data: written for this concept, not scraped from anywhere.

reviews.author: M.
reviews.text: My daughter is terrified of the dark and she walked out of the screening asking for a firefly nightlight. The scene where Luma admits she's scared to the whole swarm had me in bits.

reviews.author: D.
reviews.text: Visually the most beautiful thing they've screened in years. That single warm light drifting over the dark field is going to be on posters forever.

reviews.author: A.
reviews.text: Took my mum. She cried, I cried, the man next to us cried. It's about being the one everyone depends on when you feel smallest. Middle sagged a little but the ending lands.

reviews.author: R.
reviews.text: Went in expecting a kids film about bugs. Got a film about my own anxiety. The harvest lights going out is one of the great openings.

reviews.author: J.
reviews.text: Kids were glued. As a parent I appreciated it never talks down to them, it lets the scary bits actually be a bit scary.

reviews.author: S.
reviews.text: The octopus copying the confident kids to fit in destroyed me. Every shy teenager needs to see this. The swim meet finale where he stops imitating is perfect.

reviews.author: K.
reviews.text: Funniest one in ages. The ink cloud gag never got old, and my 9 year old has quoted the coach every day since.

reviews.author: T.
reviews.text: It's really about masking. As an autistic adult I did not expect a swim-team comedy to get that so right.

reviews.author: P.
reviews.text: A buddy comedy where the buddies genuinely annoy each other for 40 minutes and you still root for both. The sunflower through the scrap heap got an audible awww.

reviews.author: L.
reviews.text: My dad fixes cars and he said it's the first film that gets why people love repairing things. Junkyard looks gorgeous at sunset.

reviews.author: N.
reviews.text: Kids loved the tool jokes. I loved that it's secretly about two stubborn old colleagues learning to share. Very rewatchable.

reviews.author: G.
reviews.text: Fun but the city-hall subplot drags. Every scene with the two robots alone together is gold though.

reviews.author: E.
reviews.text: Call your grandmother after this one. The kitchen-table folding scenes at night are the most tender thing they've made.

reviews.author: H.
reviews.text: A thousand cranes, one promise, no villain. Proof you don't need a chase scene if the feelings are true. My teenagers were silent the whole ride home.
